title: API Compatibility Harness Implementation Tasks
principles: Pin the public HTTP API contract before adding broader API, SDK or app surface area.
Generate compatibility evidence from the same @taxkit/api-http contracts that serve production routes.
Keep apps/api as the process and live smoke owner; keep packages/api/http as the HTTP contract, OpenAPI and route fixture owner.
Reuse calculator-owned schemas, SDK facades and tagged errors instead of mirroring DTOs in tests or smoke code.
Prefer deterministic structured snapshots and focused fixtures over brittle full-response churn.
Require parent review, final call-graph verification and three documented improvement audit passes before accepting each implementation slice.
beforeImplementation: Create an active execution plan under docs/exec-plans/active/ before editing runtime, package scripts or package docs.
Record the current OpenAPI generation path, current route tests and current app smoke commands before changing files.
Implement one task at a time with parent review before starting the next task.
Do not run bun run version-repo unless the user explicitly asks for release versioning.

globalVerification.commitPolicy: Commit after each coherent implementation slice passes mandatory verification, Changeset review or no-Changeset rationale, and parent audit.
requiredBeforeFinalPR: bun run changeset status --verbose when a Changeset exists, or explicit no-Changeset rationale for internal test/script/docs-only slices
evidenceRequired: Current and final call graph summary for OpenAPI generation, in-process route fixture tests and live apps/api smoke coverage
OpenAPI snapshot path and update command
Route fixture coverage matrix for health, metadata, calculate success and schema-guided input error behaviour
SDK parity evidence for calculate success and expected input errors
Live API app smoke command output
Changeset path and release-train impact, or explicit no-Changeset rationale
Parent audit outcome for each task, including up to three correction turns when needed

tasks.title: Add the OpenAPI snapshot harness
tasks.goal: Make OpenAPI drift visible by generating a deterministic snapshot from the same @taxkit/api-http contract used by /api/docs/openapi.json.
scope: Inspect packages/api/http/src/server/live.layer.ts, packages/api/http/src/api.ts and route groups before editing.
Extract the current OpenApi.fromApi(TaxKitApi) path into a package-owned OpenAPI source, such as packages/api/http/src/openapi.ts.
Make /api/docs/openapi.json import the same OpenAPI source used by compatibility tests.
Add a deterministic OpenAPI normalizer that uses structured JSON values and stable ordering rather than string rewriting.
Commit a normalized OpenAPI snapshot under packages/api/http, for example packages/api/http/__snapshots__/openapi.json.
Add a package test or focused package command that compares the generated current OpenAPI document to the committed snapshot.
Add a documented snapshot update workflow for intentional contract changes.
outputs: packages/api/http/src/openapi.ts or equivalent package-owned OpenAPI source
packages/api/http/package.json if a focused compatibility or update script is added

mandatorySubagentContract: You are implementing exactly one task from the TaxKit spec/task list.
Before editing, read the target spec, this task object, relevant current files, and the relevant docs in docs/architecture/*.
Identify the owning package for every schema, type, ID, service, layer and tagged error you touch.
Review the spec's call-graph diagrams before editing, and update or report them if implementation discovers a different final graph.
MUST use Effect-native primitives when they fit: Data, Schema, Array, Chunk, HashSet, HashMap, Match, Context, Layer, Config, Service, Record, Result, Exit, Bun, Platform, Command and ManagedRuntime.

Do not fall back to plain TypeScript control flow, nullable values, mutable records, mutable arrays, switch, Object.values, Object.entries, ad hoc _tag objects, unsafe casts, stringly branching, custom env parsing or hand-rolled runtime wrappers when an Effect/schema/platform primitive fits.
Reuse canonical schema-derived types, branded IDs, tagged errors, service contracts and constructors from the owning package.
Do not create mirrored DTOs, local duplicate types, local duplicate schemas or transport-only shape copies when an owning schema or API/SDK contract already exists.

Keep one-off Effect error handling and transformations inline at the callsite. Do not extract tiny mapper/wrapper helpers for single-use Effect.mapError, Effect.catchTag, Effect.catchAll or Effect.catchAllDefect.
Browser/runtime code must consume browser-safe API/SDK exports instead of importing server-only internals.
Package-facing changes MUST add or update a Changeset with bun run changeset, using a user-facing changelog summary. If the task is not package-facing, state that explicitly in the handoff.
Run this task's mandatory verification gates, including bun run verification unless the task explicitly documents a narrower gate.
Run task-specific tests, smoke checks, browser checks or architecture audits required by the task's blast radius.
For substantial code, API, SDK, app, package-boundary or docs-runtime work, run and document at least three implementation improvement audit passes before handoff.
Audit the diff for helper sprawl, canonical type/schema/id/error reuse, unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, stringly branching and browser-safe imports where relevant.
Report the Changeset path and release-train impact, or report why no Changeset was required.
Report changed files, verification commands, outcomes and residual risks.
Report whether the final implementation still matches the spec's call graph.
Do not start or delegate another task. The parent agent must review, audit, verify and explicitly accept this task before the next task begins.
taskSpecificScope: Do not handwrite OpenAPI JSON as the contract source.
Do not duplicate OpenAPI generation between the docs route and snapshot test.
Do not redesign routes, status envelopes or calculator schemas.
Keep snapshot normalization deterministic and structured.

mandatoryVerification: OpenAPI snapshot drift audit: prove the test compares current generated OpenAPI to the committed snapshot and would fail on an unapproved route, method or status-schema change.
rg audit proving /api/docs/openapi.json and the snapshot harness share the same package-owned OpenAPI source.
bun run changeset or explicit no-Changeset rationale for internal test/snapshot/docs-only work
browserVerification: No browser-visible UI behaviour is expected to change.
No browser bundle should import server-only OpenAPI or handler modules.
qualityAudits: Audit pass 1: OpenAPI generation call graph is single-source and still matches the spec.
Audit pass 2: snapshot normalization uses structured data, stable ordering and no local DTO mirrors or handwritten route shapes.
Audit pass 3: Effect/schema conventions, canonical API contracts, no unsafe casts, no helper sprawl and no browser/server import leaks.
completionCriteria: The OpenAPI snapshot is committed under packages/api/http.
The docs route and snapshot harness share one package-owned OpenAPI generation path.
The package test fails on unapproved OpenAPI drift.
The update workflow for intentional OpenAPI changes is documented.
Parent agent reviewed the diff, snapshot, verification evidence, Changeset decision and final call graph before accepting the task.

tasks.title: Add route fixtures and SDK parity coverage
tasks.goal: Pin representative public route responses and keep calculate behaviour aligned with the SDK and calculator-owned errors.
scope: Inspect the existing packages/api/http/__tests__/public-calculation-api.test.ts before editing.
Add route fixture coverage for GET /api/health.
Add route fixture coverage for at least one metadata route, preferably GET /api/v1/calculators.
Add calculate success fixture coverage for POST /api/v1/calculators/au.pay.take-home/calculate.
Add schema-guided input error fixture coverage for a mismatched or missing take-home input fact.
Keep or strengthen the existing HTTP response equals SDK full-run response assertion.
Compare expected input errors with SDK/calculator-owned tagged errors rather than duplicating local error DTOs.
Use canonical schemas, branded IDs, tagged values and rule-owned constructors in fixtures where the test boundary permits it.
outputs: packages/api/http/__fixtures__/**/*.json or focused fixture assertions in test files
packages/api/http/README.md if fixture/update workflow is documented there

taskSpecificScope: Do not create local DTO mirrors for calculator catalog, run responses or error envelopes.
Do not snapshot huge unstable traces if focused fixture assertions provide stronger compatibility evidence.
Do not change public route behaviour to make tests easier.
Keep calculate success aligned with @taxkit/sdk/effect calculateRunRequest.

mandatoryVerification: Route fixture coverage audit documenting health, metadata, calculate success and schema-guided input error coverage.
SDK parity audit proving calculate success and expected input error evidence still flows through canonical SDK/calculator contracts.
bun run changeset or explicit no-Changeset rationale for internal test/fixture/docs-only work
browserVerification: No browser-visible UI behaviour is expected to change.
If browser-safe API client exports are touched, audit apps/web imports and run bun run --filter=web check-types.
qualityAudits: Audit pass 1: fixture call graph still proves HTTP over SDK and calculator-owned errors.
Audit pass 2: fixtures reuse canonical schemas, branded IDs, tagged constructors and service errors with no local DTO mirrors.
Audit pass 3: Effect test flow, inline error handling, no unsafe casts beyond documented external-input boundary tests, no helper sprawl and no browser/server import leaks.
completionCriteria: Route fixtures cover health, metadata, calculate success and schema-guided input error behaviour.
Calculate success still compares HTTP output to the SDK full-run response.
Expected input error evidence remains aligned with SDK and calculator-owned tagged errors.
No fixture or test file introduces duplicated canonical response DTOs.
Parent agent reviewed the diff, fixture coverage matrix, verification evidence, Changeset decision and final call graph before accepting the task.

tasks.title: Add live API app smoke and documentation
tasks.goal: Prove the standalone apps/api process serves the pinned public routes and document the compatibility workflow.
scope: Inspect apps/api/src/index.ts, apps/api/src/server.ts, apps/api/src/config.ts and apps/api/README.md before editing.
Add an app-owned smoke command, such as bun run --filter=api smoke:public-routes.

The smoke command should start apps/api with a deterministic host and port, wait for /api/health, call /api/v1/calculators, call /api/v1/calculators/au.pay.take-home/calculate, call /api/docs/openapi.json and stop the process cleanly.
Use Effect platform/runtime primitives for command execution, timing, cleanup and expected errors where they fit.
Keep smoke code app-owned. Do not move process lifecycle into @taxkit/api-http.
Update apps/api/README.md with the smoke command and public route coverage.
Update packages/api/http/README.md with OpenAPI snapshot, route fixture and intentional contract-change workflow.
Record final compatibility evidence and release-train impact.
outputs: apps/api/scripts/smoke-public-routes.ts or equivalent app-owned smoke script
Any docs/product-specs status updates made after implementation

taskSpecificScope: Do not duplicate apps/api runtime ownership inside packages/api/http.
Do not leave child processes running after smoke failures.
Do not use a live network dependency outside the local API process.
Do not broaden documentation into generated docs inventory work.

mandatoryVerification: Live smoke cleanup audit proving the command stops apps/api on success and failure.
README audit proving compatibility commands and intentional contract-change workflow are documented.
bun run changeset status --verbose when a Changeset exists, or explicit no-Changeset rationale for app-internal/docs-only smoke tooling
browserVerification: No browser-visible UI behaviour is expected to change.
No browser check is required unless implementation touches apps/web, apps/docs routes or browser-safe API client exports.
qualityAudits: Audit pass 1: live smoke call graph keeps apps/api as runtime owner and @taxkit/api-http as route contract owner.
Audit pass 2: smoke process lifecycle uses Effect platform/runtime primitives where they fit and always cleans up.
Audit pass 3: docs are compact, current and linked to architecture docs; no local DTO mirrors, unsafe casts, helper sprawl or package-boundary leaks are introduced.
completionCriteria: apps/api exposes and passes a live public-route smoke command.
The smoke command calls health, metadata, calculate and OpenAPI routes against a real local apps/api process.
The smoke command cleans up the local API process on success and failure.
apps/api and packages/api/http READMEs document compatibility commands and intentional contract-change workflow.
Final package, app, docs and repo verification gates pass.
Parent agent reviewed the diff, smoke output, README updates, release impact and final call graph before accepting the task.
